name: Halvah- Chocolate Mousse Napoleon
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound unsalted margarine, at room temperature
1 pound halvah cut into 1/2 inch slices to fit molds
recipeInstructions:
1. Serve with chocolate sauce (recipe follows).
2. Melt the chocolate over simmering water in a double boiler.
3. Let it cool to room temperature.
4. In a large bowl, beat chocolate and margarine until light and fluffy.
5. Add lemon juice and beat together.
6. In another bowl, beat egg whites until soft peaks form, add sugar and beat until stiff but not dry.
7. Fold the egg white mixture into the chocolate mixture.
8. Spoon the chocolate mixture into a pastry bag fitted with a plain round tip.
9. Line two 3x7 inch molds with parchment paper, allowing enough to hang over edges.
10. Arrange slices of halvah on bottom of each mold.
11. Pipe a single layer of mousse about 1/2 inch thick over the halvah.
12. Cover another layer of halvah and pipe second layer of mousse.
13. Repeat layers until you have three layers of halvah and two layers of mousse.
14. Cover with parchment paper and refrigerate overnight.
15. About 30 minutes before serving, invert mold, release parchment, and cut into slices.
16. Place on chilled dessert plates, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate.
17. Just before serving, spoon chocolate sauce onto one side of the mousse.
18. Garnish with mint leaves and fresh strawberries.
Chocolate Sauce:
8 ounces semisweet chocolate, coarsely chopped
1/2 cup strong hot coffee
1/2 cup apricot or strawberry preserves, strained
1 tablespoon fruit liqueur (optional)
In a double boiler over simmering water, melt chocolate, coffee, preserves, and liqueur. Mix constantly until melted and well blended. Transfer to a glass bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate. Serve hot or cold.
